Niagara Assembly Center · 2025
Geographic Strategy

Roadmap

A phased geographic strategy from Hamilton anchor to binational network.

1
Phase 1 — Now
Hamilton Anchor

NAC's inaugural facility in Hamilton, Ontario will serve as the operational and strategic nerve center of the network. Hamilton's industrial heritage, available commercial real estate, post-secondary institutions, and transit infrastructure make it the natural anchor for a precision electronics operation.

  • 4,000–8,000 sq. ft. facility configured for end-to-end small-batch assembly, test, and packaging
  • IPC-A-610 compliant assembly environment with ESD controls, cleanroom-adjacent workflow zones
  • In-house firmware development lab for embedded systems optimization
  • Training and demonstration space for workforce and partner education programs
  • Administrative and network coordination hub for the broader NAC ecosystem
2
Phase 2 — Near-Term
Fort Erie Foothold

Fort Erie represents NAC's most strategically important early expansion. Proximity to the Peace Bridge crossing provides direct operational continuity with the Buffalo-Niagara market. NAC will establish office and hosting facilities in Fort Erie to serve both Ontario and New York State actors — a physical presence that signals binational commitment and facilitates the legal and logistical structures required for cross-border production flows.

3
Phase 3 — Expansion
Buffalo-Niagara Registration

NAC will register as a US entity in New York State from the outset of operations, with first-phase relationships centered on SUNY Buffalo's electrical engineering and computer science programs on the North Campus. These academic partnerships enable access to research capabilities, co-op talent pipelines, and START-UP NY benefits including tax abatements and subsidized operational costs for partnering companies.

NYPA's low-cost hydropower rates available to eligible manufacturers in Western New York represent a meaningful operational cost advantage for energy-intensive assembly and test operations — a differentiated input cost that strengthens NAC's competitive positioning against offshore alternatives.
